paper_title: On heroes in digraphs with forbidden induced forests
summary: Question 1.1 — the overarching problem of classifying all finite families F of digraphs that are vec{chi}-finite — remains open. The source paper (published in European Journal of Combinatorics, 2025) contributes positive results for several specific forbidden families involving forests. Subsequent work (arXiv:2602.08736, 2026) shows that the family {claw, directed-C_3} is NOT vec{chi}-finite, adding to the map of negative cases, but no paper provides a general characterization.

since_posted.title: (Claw, C_3)-free digraphs with unbounded dichromatic number
since_posted.claim: Constructs claw-free digraphs with no directed 3-cycle but unbounded dichromatic number, disproving a conjecture of Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr and improving a negative result of Carbonero–Koerts–Moore–Spirkl, thereby showing {claw, directed-C_3} is not vec{chi}-finite.

search_queries: chi-finite digraphs dichromatic number heroes forbidden induced arXiv 2306.04710
dichromatic number chi-finite family F-free digraphs heroes Carbonero Koerts Moore Spirkl 2023 2024 2025
vec chi-finite families digraphs dichromatic number 2024 2025 heroes Carbonero Spirkl arXiv
notes: Question 1.1 is explicitly framed as the overarching open problem of the paper and is not a conjecture with a specific claimed answer, making it inherently long-term open. The source paper was published in European Journal of Combinatorics 125 (2025), article 104104 (confirmed via citation in arXiv:2602.08736). No paper found that resolves or substantially narrows the full classification.
